indication: Chronic low back pain with bilateral lower extremity numbness and tingling, history of diabetic neuropathy, evaluate for spinal stenosis and nerve root compression
technique: Multiplanar MRI of the lumbar spine was performed without contrast administration. Sequences include sagittal T1, T2, and STIR, and axial T1 and T2 at L3-L4, L4-L5, and L5-S1.

findings.vertebralBodies: Normal marrow signal. Normal vertebral body heights maintained. No compression fracture. No suspicious osseous lesion.
findings.conus: Conus medullaris terminates at the L1 level, normal in position and signal.
findings.l1l2: No disc herniation. Mild disc desiccation. No significant canal or foraminal stenosis.
findings.l2l3: Mild disc bulge with mild disc desiccation. No significant canal stenosis. No foraminal stenosis.
findings.l3l4: Mild-to-moderate disc bulge with disc desiccation. Mild bilateral facet arthropathy. Mild central canal stenosis (AP diameter 10 mm). No significant foraminal stenosis.
findings.l4l5: Moderate broad-based disc bulge with moderate disc desiccation and loss of disc height. Moderate bilateral facet arthropathy with hypertrophy. Moderate central canal stenosis (AP diameter 8 mm). Mild bilateral foraminal stenosis. Mild encroachment on the bilateral L5 nerve roots.
findings.l5s1: Moderate disc desiccation with loss of disc height and vacuum phenomenon. Mild-to-moderate broad-based disc bulge. Moderate bilateral facet arthropathy. Mild central canal stenosis (AP diameter 9 mm). Mild right foraminal stenosis with possible contact of the right S1 nerve root.
findings.paraspinalSoftTissues: Unremarkable. No paraspinal mass or collection.

impression: Multilevel degenerative disc disease with the most significant findings at L4-L5.
Moderate central canal stenosis at L4-L5 (AP diameter 8 mm) with bilateral facet arthropathy and mild bilateral foraminal stenosis with encroachment on the L5 nerve roots.
Mild central canal stenosis at L3-L4 and L5-S1.
Mild right foraminal stenosis at L5-S1 with possible contact of the right S1 nerve root.
No compression fracture, spondylolisthesis, or suspicious osseous lesion.
Note: Patient's lower extremity symptoms may be multifactorial, including contributions from spinal stenosis and known diabetic neuropathy.
recommendations: Clinical correlation with lower extremity symptoms and neurological examination
Consider neurosurgery or pain management consultation if symptoms are primarily related to spinal stenosis
EMG/NCS may help differentiate spinal stenosis from diabetic neuropathy as the primary etiology
comparison: Lumbar spine X-ray dated 2019-09-14 showed degenerative disc disease at L4-L5 and L5-S1. MRI provides more detailed assessment confirming stenosis.
